customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high engagement. Corey is a self-motivated researcher who submitted the form, is actively comparing quotes, and has been reading about solar financing independently. His cooperative posture held throughout the call. Primary risk is not skepticism — it's the email-quote promise Joe made.
unresolved_concerns: Tax credit / deferred cost structure — Corey asked specifically about this and received a bill-swap explanation that answered adjacent to his question, not directly.
Payback timeline — Corey asked how long it takes to pay off a system; Joe correctly deferred but did not bridge to the closer.
Co-owner status unknown — Joe never asked if anyone else is on the title. If a spouse or partner is co-owner, they are not included in the 4PM appointment.
sensitive_topics_raised: Competing Sunday quote from another company — Corey volunteered this; no differentiation framing was planted before that appointment.
exact_promises_rep_made: We'll send you an email as well — confirmed to coryHoffman757@gmail.com. This is a protocol violation; Ion's policy is not to email proposals before the meeting. Closer must address this at the top of the call.
Closer will show a side-by-side comparison of Dominion costs vs. solar savings.
closer_should_know: Joe promised to email a quote before the meeting — Ion's policy prohibits this. The closer must address this promise at the top of the call before Corey asks where the email is. Suggested framing: 'Joe mentioned we'd follow up by email — what we actually do is walk through the full design live so nothing gets lost in translation; that's what today is.' Corey is warm and explicitly appreciated Joe's honesty, so a direct, calm correction will land. Do not ignore the promise and hope Corey forgot.
